metadata.title:
.HENEY ON HALLS TRAIL

TRIAL OF JOHN HALL, FORMER
V. S. ATTORNEY FOR LAND
FRAUDS, BEGINS AT PORT
LOND TODAY.

pages.raw_text:
PORTLAND. Jan. 13.-The first
land fraud trial called in Portland
under direction of Heney for over
year opened this morning before
Federal Judge Hunt, when John
Hall, former United States district
attorney, charged with illegally
fencing government lands, was
brought up for trial. With Hall is
associated Winlock w. Steiwer as
co-defendant.


Hall is charged with conspiracy
for illegal fencing in which 1880
acres of public lands were inclosed
and used by livestock corpora
ton as pasture. The morning ses-
sion was consumed in preliminary
motions.


Veniremen were ordered to re-
port at court at 2 o'clock in the aft
ernoon for examination. Heney
charged Hall with obstructing him
In his work of rounding up land
frauders and finally had him dis
missed from Office. Hall has al
ways maintained he was innocent.


1t is expected that the trial Will
last about two weeks and that fully
80 witnesses will be examined


A motion for a separate trial
made by John Hall over two years
ago before the late Judge Bellinger
and a motion for a plea in abate-
ment made by Franklin p. Hays al
the same time, We're found on the
docket this morning by Judge
Hunt and will have to be disposed
of before Francis J. Heney can get
down to the real work of trying
Hall on the charge of conspiracy
and land fraud. The motions had
been forgotten by both sides.


Judge Bellinger died before he
ruled upon them and the eases had
dragged ever since that time. Both
Hall and Hays took until tomorrow
to decide what steps they would
take in regard to the motion.
